For your situation in Makueni, drought-resistant crops like sorghum, millet, green grams, and mangoes tend to perform best because rainfall is limited.
Tomatoes is profitable because it has steady demand and multiple harvest cycles per season.
Onions is profitable because it has consistent demand in urban markets and generally strong prices.
Okra is profitable because it has high demand in both local and export markets with good margins.
- Rotate crops and test soil to keep yields high.

what crop should I seell in Makueni?

Based on current market trends, Tomatoes, Onions and Okra are in high demand in Nairobi, Mombasa, Kisumu. Prices are moving with demand due to strong demand and export demand.

You can sell tomatoes, onions, or okra in urban markets, as they move quickly due to daily consumption.
Best places to sell:
- Wakulima Market (Nairobi)
- Gikomba Market (Nairobi)
- Kongowea Market (Mombasa)
- Kibuye Market (Kisumu)
Tip: These crops sell fast when brought early in the morning, and buyers pay more for fresh, well-sorted produce.
What should i plant in makueni?

Based on current market trends, Onions, Avocado, Okra and Macadamia are currently in high demand in Kenyan urban markets. Prices are moving with demand due to export demand.

For your situation in Makueni, drought-resistant crops like sorghum, millet, green grams, and mangoes tend to perform best because rainfall is limited and these crops hold value even in dry seasons.
Sorghum is profitable because it has drought tolerant and in demand where rainfall is low.
Millet is profitable because it has resilient in dry conditions and valued in local diets.
Green grams is profitable because it has fast-maturing and profitable in drier zones.
Mangoes is profitable because it has strong local demand and good margins.
Onions is profitable because it has consistent demand in urban markets and generally strong prices.
Avocado is profitable because it has export demand keeps prices high, especially in dry seasons.
Okra is profitable because it has high demand in both local and export markets with good margins.
Macadamia is profitable because it has high export value when mature, suitable for investment crops.
- Rotate crops and test soil to keep yields high.
